text: 1. Through low-temperature rolling, a novel lamellar steel composed of δ-ferrite lamellae and carbide/ferrite lamellae has been prepared. A notable feature of this steel is the presence of numerous nanoscale spherical carbides within the carbide/ferrite lamellae. These carbides inhibit crack propagation during impact and tensile processes, endowing the tested steel with excellent toughness $( \mathrm { I A E } = 2 0 7 . 5 \pm 2 9 . 3 \mathrm { J } )$ . While reducing the density of the experimental steel, this approach meets the requirement for improved mechanical properties, providing a new strategy for enhancing the mechanical performance of δ-ferrite lightweight steel.

2. The improvement of the tested steel's toughness by the carbide/ ferrite lamellae is closely related to the size and distribution density of the spherical carbides. The ultrafine spherical carbides in R650 exhibit a smaller size $( 1 5 8 . 3 \pm 7 6 . 9$ nm) and a higher distribution density (1.7 ± 0.2 μm− 2 ), resulting in significantly higher toughness compared to those of R750 (size: $2 4 7 . 3 \pm 8 6 . 8$ nm; distribution density: $0 . 6 \pm 0 . 1 ~ \mu \mathrm { m } ^ { - 2 } )$ . A dual-phase structure consisting of δ-ferrite lamellae and honeycomb martensite lamellae was obtained via rolling in the high-temperature rolling region. Numerous equiaxed ferrite grains exist in the honeycomb martensite lamellae, which act as “crack propagation shortcuts” for pre-existing cracks, accelerating crack propagation and significantly deteriorating the toughness of the tested steel. # CRediT authorship contribution statement Ming Chen: Writing – original draft, Methodology, Investigation, Data curation. Feng Hu: Writing – review & editing, Resources, Investigation, Conceptualization. Songbo Zhou: Writing – review & editing, Software, Methodology. Serhii Yershov: Supervision, Resources, Funding acquisition. Kaiming Wu: Supervision, Project administration, Funding acquisition. # Declaration of competing interest The authors declare the following financial interests/personal relationships which may be considered as potential competing interests: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. # Acknowledgements The authors are grateful for the financial support to the Science and Technology Program of Hubei Province (2024AFB401), Science and Technology Research Project of Hubei Provincial Department of Education (F2023021) and National Key Research and Development Program of China (2022YFB4201502).
